input.task: What kinds of technical debt are mentioned, and what practices help prevent them?

content.answer_preview: The mentioned technical debts are Configuration debt, Dependency debt, and Data testing debt. Prevention practices for these debts include pinning dependencies (to prevent configuration debt), using version control (for enabling experiment tracking), setting up Continuous Integration (to catch integration problems early), documenting data contracts (to prevent dependency debt), and implementing schema validation (to prevent data testing debt). (Page 74, 76)

final_answer: The mentioned technical debts are Configuration debt, Dependency debt, and Data testing debt. Prevention practices for these debts include pinning dependencies (to prevent configuration debt), using version control (for enabling experiment tracking), setting up Continuous Integration (to catch integration problems early), documenting data contracts (to prevent dependency debt), and implementing schema validation (to prevent data testing debt). (Page 74, 76)
